name: Documentation & Proof System
description: Captures evidence of transformation for: learner motivation (see your own progress), quality assurance (verify the system works), and marketing validation (testimonials with proof).

what_to_capture.task: Record 60 seconds: introduce yourself, talk about your family and daily routine.
Read aloud: the standard passage from Module 1 placement (beginner level).
Write 5 sentences about yourself (no help, no translator).
Read aloud: 10 target words containing this week's phonemes.
Rate yourself 1-10 on: speaking confidence, listening ability, vocabulary, pronunciation, writing.

baseline_capture.delivery_message:
🏛️ **BASELINE RECORDING — Day 1**

Before you begin, we need to capture where you are RIGHT NOW.
In 8 weeks, you'll compare these recordings to your progress. The difference will surprise you.

Please record:
1. 📢 **60 seconds speaking**: Tell me your name, where you're from, your family, your daily routine, and your goal. (No script — just speak!)
2. 📖 **Read aloud**: [provided passage]
3. ✍️ **Write 5 sentences** about yourself

Send all three here. This is for YOUR progress tracking — not graded!
baseline_capture.storage: Google Drive folder (founder-managed) or Discord DM archive. Never publicly shared without explicit consent.

schedule.content: Introduction, reading passage, writing sample, self-assessment
Same format + advancement speech if applicable

progress_recordings.reminder_system: n8n workflow or Discord bot reminds member to record at each milestone. Provides the exact same prompts as Day 1 for direct comparison.
progress_recordings.comparison_delivery: After each recording, bot sends the baseline recording BACK to the learner with: 'Listen to Day 1 you. Then listen to today. Notice the difference.'

access_rules.public: Only with explicit opt-in consent (for marketing/testimonials)

recording_archive.implementation_pilot: Store in dedicated Google Drive folder shared with founder. Discord messages also serve as archive (searchable by user).
recording_archive.implementation_scale: Dedicated storage (Cloudflare R2 or S3) with metadata in database.

story_format.before: Where were you before? What was your biggest struggle?
story_format.the_decision: What made you start? What pushed you?
story_format.the_process: What was your daily routine like? What was hardest?
story_format.the_breakthrough: When did you first notice a difference? What moment?
story_format.after: Where are you now? What can you do that you couldn't before?
story_format.the_proof: Specific, measurable evidence (scores, duration, situations handled)
story_format.advice: What would you tell someone who's thinking of starting?

collection_method.pilot: Founder interviews the learner (voice call, 10 min). Transcribes key quotes.
collection_method.scale: Bot sends structured prompts at milestones. Learner writes/records responses.
collection_method.consent: Explicit opt-in required before any story is shared publicly. Granular: 'Use my story?' + 'Use my name?' + 'Use my recording?'

usage: Internal quality validation (does the system actually produce results?)

30_day_comparison_message.template:
🎉 **30-DAY MILESTONE — {NAME}!**
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

You've been learning for 30 days. Let's see how far you've come.

📢 **Task:** Record yourself again — same prompt as Day 1:
"Tell me your name, where you're from, your family, your daily routine, and your goal."
→ Target: 60 seconds
→ Send recording here

After you record, I'll send you your Day 1 recording.
Listen to both back-to-back.

The difference? That's 30 days of consistency. 🏛️

📊 Your numbers:
• Tasks completed: {TASKS}
• Current streak: {STREAK} days
• Average score: {AVG_SCORE}
• Vocabulary learned: ~{VOCAB_ESTIMATE} words
• Speaking recordings submitted: {SPEAK_COUNT}

You're building something real. Keep going. 💪

data_privacy.recordings_owned_by: The learner (they can request deletion at any time)
data_privacy.retention: 12 months after last activity, then archived. Permanently deleted on request.
consent_required_for: Sharing with other learners as example
